content: Kicked off the DevManiac Android client. Empty Compose scaffold, Hilt wired, Retrofit pointed at staging. First milestone is the read-only feed.
Settled the module layout: :app, :core:network, :core:designsystem, :feature:feed. Kept it boring on purpose — four modules is enough until build times say otherwise.
Feed screen renders real staging data. Shimmer placeholders, pull-to-refresh, and the event cards match the web design closely enough that I had to double-check which one I was looking at.
Replaced hand-rolled cursor pagination with Paging 3. Deleted ~200 lines and fixed the duplicate-item flash on refresh in the same move.

problem_solutions.problem: Items flashed duplicates when a refresh landed mid-scroll because the old cursor list and new page briefly overlapped.
problem_solutions.solution: Moved to Paging 3 with a keyed PagingSource; DiffUtil keys on event id so overlapping pages reconcile instead of double-rendering.

content: Lost half a day to a serialization crash: the backend sends event_metadata as an arbitrary JSON object and my DTO had it as Map<String, String>. One nested object in production data and the whole feed page failed to parse.

problem_solutions.problem: kotlinx.serialization threw on event_metadata because nested objects don't fit Map<String, String>.
problem_solutions.solution: Typed the field as JsonObject and let each card reader pull out the keys it knows about. Unknown shapes now degrade gracefully instead of killing the page.

content: Milestone: comments are live. Two-level threading, optimistic upvotes, and reply composer with @-mention completion. That's the last read-write surface before I start the profile screen.
